title: How windshields can affect tinted eyeglasses
author: John Paul
bullets: Photochromic lenses do not darken in cars due to UV light filtering by laminated windshields.
Modern vehicle windshields provide UV protection and contribute to structural rigidity and safety features.
No current vehicles allow photochromic lenses to activate through windshields due to UV filtering.

title: Mass. police department cancels appearance of Trump-backed ‘Freedom Truck’ over ties to right-wing organization
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Westfield Police Department canceled Freedom Truck event due to concerns about partisan affiliations.
Freedom Truck is backed by Trump and PragerU, receives federal funding, and is part of Freedom 250 initiative.
Event withdrawal follows other Freedom 250-related cancellations, including artists pulling out of the Great American State Fair.

title: DA: Man charged in Pittsfield fatal stabbing had ‘no prior connection’ to victim
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Zyrus Jaynes, 24, charged with murder for allegedly fatally stabbing Jesse Gray in Pittsfield on May 29.
Prosecutors stated Gray, 36, and Jaynes had no prior connection before the altercation outside a multi-unit home.
Jaynes previously faced multiple assault charges in 2025; he pleaded not guilty to the current murder charge.

title: Patriots lose new tight end for 2026 season due to injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Julian Hill placed on injured reserve, ruling him out for the 2026 Patriots season.
Hill signed a three-year, $15 million contract as a backup tight end behind Hunter Henry.
2026 third-round pick Eli Raridon may fill Hill's role unless more roster changes occur.

title: 5 clues A.J. Brown dropped about his desire to join the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown expressed disappointment when the Patriots chose N'Keal Harry over him in the 2019 draft.
In 2024, Brown changed his Instagram profile picture to Tom Brady in a Patriots jersey, sparking trade inquiries.
Brown showed excitement after receiving a signed Patriots jersey from Brady, indicating his admiration for the team.

title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers reject ‘last and final’ contract proposal, plan to announce strike date
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers rejected management's contract offer, citing issues with wages and scheduling, after two years of negotiations.
Sixty-seven percent of Coolidge Corner Theatre Union members voted against the theater's 'last and final' contract proposal.
Union officials plan to announce a strike date following the vote, while management maintains their offer supports long-term stability.

title: 5 things to know about new Patriots wide receiver A.J. Brown
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots traded a 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round pick for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Brown, a former Super Bowl winner with the Eagles, has consistently surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in six of seven seasons.
Brown, originally from Mississippi, was a childhood Patriots fan and has a strong past relationship with coach Mike Vrabel.

title: Patriots acquire A.J. Brown in blockbuster trade with Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade for A.J. Brown, sending 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round picks to Eagles.
A.J. Brown has six 1,000-yard seasons in seven years, including four with Eagles.
Patriots bolster offense with Brown, addressing need after parting with Stefon Diggs in March.

title: The funeral of Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr., in photos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Thousands attended Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr.'s funeral at Cathedral of the Holy Cross on June 1, 2026.
Kilduff died in a Dorchester fire on May 23 after warning fellow firefighters of a structural danger.
Boston officials, family, and friends, including Mayor Michelle Wu, commemorated his bravery and dedication at the service.

title: A ‘fishy squisher’: NASA confirms meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: NASA confirms a meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay after a sonic boom over Massachusetts.
The meteor released energy equivalent to 300 tons of TNT, breaking up 40 miles above New England.
The American Meteor Society received over 80 reports from witnesses as far as Ontario, Canada.
